danger_signals.meaning: Hazards requiring dual weapon response (projectiles vs bombs)

danger_signals.visual_form: Large antagonistic sprite at stage end
Life bar depletion at bottom-left screen
danger_signals.meaning: Dragon Amur health status; two-hit tolerance before death
Paleozoic world-state danger thematically tied to terrain type

danger_signals.visual_form: Zawell (serpent demon) as final boss visual
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat to kingdom; source of kidnapping threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue spherical collectible from destroyed eggs
safety_signals.meaning: Extra dragon head grants additional firepower; beneficial state expansion
Shot strength enhancement; offensive capability boost
Homing shot, multi-shot, shrink power — situational survival tools

safety_signals.visual_form: Character rescue sprite shown after area completion
safety_signals.meaning: Rescue confirmation; story progression and conditional aid (Blue Dragon route only)
Progression safety net; death recovery without full stage restart

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Kingdom celebration scenes in ending; peaceful landscape restoration

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Serpent demon Zawell visual; boss sprite design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Minion enemies (air and ground-based types) spawned across nine stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corrupted/hostile stage environments (volcanoes, frozen wastelands); captivity glass chamber holding Princess Alicia

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nine stages filled with enemy spawns, volcanic/frozen/jungle corrupted terrain, Princess Alicia imprisoned behind glass
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy waves; boss confrontations at stage ends; Amur must use dual weapons to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lightning strike effect on Amur; human form converts to dragon form via supernatural event
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains flight capability, dual weapon system (projectiles + bombs), multi-hit health pool

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Glass shatters; Alicia visible and vocal; Amur retransforms to human; kingdom celebration cutscene
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy threat eliminated; Zawell defeated; peaceful restoration narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Maiden appears as shadow or solid form depending on power conditions (firepower level, dragon head count, random chance)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Conditional aid delivery (energy restoration, 1-up grants) only when specific thresholds met; story unchanged but mechanical advantage varies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage biomes: large rivers, volcanoes, glaciers, jungles — prehistoric landscape aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Terrain-specific enemy types and hazard patterns; thematic coherence to ancient world narrative

environmental_storytelling.example: Nine distinct biomes (river → volcano → glacier → jungle progression) show world-state corruption under Zawell's reign; final stage reaches demon's lair
Princess Alicia shown behind shattering glass at stage-end cutscene; imprisonment reinforces tyranny before rescue completes
Opening cutscene: Amur raises sword on mountain, lightning strikes, human becomes dragon — establishes magical intervention as salvation mechanism
End scene shows kingdom celebrating peace and prosperity after Zawell's defeat; visual statement of tyranny-to-peace transition
Each stage boss represents Zawell's lieutenants/corrupted forces; defeating them weakens tyranny progressively toward final confrontation
US version adds shirt to dream cutscene character (censorship); removes upskirt code at ending — signals cultural/political messaging differences in world-state interpretation across regions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves in eight directions; fires projectiles at air targets; deploys bombs at ground targets; takes two hits before death
Increased firepower output; capable of dealing with denser enemy waves; visual multiple-head sprite indicates enhanced state
Homing shots track enemies; multi-shot creates spread pattern; shrink power enables evasion of otherwise unavoidable bullets — adaptive response to danger levels
Spawns in waves; requires projectile weapon response; indicates aerial threat zone
Positioned on terrain; requires bomb weapon response; indicates ground-zone threat, often near egg pickups
Dual weapon vulnerability; mandatory defeat for stage progression; visual size/design indicates individual threat hierarchy
Commands entire nine-stage enemy infrastructure; final combat gate; defeat triggers rescue narrative completion and transformation back to human form
Static captivity behind glass; vocal reaction ('Amur!') when glass breaks; indicates goal state before rescue

Manifests only when specific thresholds met (below 3 firepower, below 3 heads, or random); delivers aid (energy/1-up); shadow form indicates threshold failure — mechanical feedback for resource management

notes: Dragon Spirit: The New Legend uses dual-mechanic weapon system (projectiles vs bombs) as core danger-response language, reinforcing threat categorization (air vs ground). Paleozoic setting provides environmental visual coherence. Regional censorship (US version shirt addition, upskirt code removal) demonstrates how world-state political/cultural messaging is manifested through content filtering. Maiden conditional appearance system uses visual manifestation (shadow vs solid form) to communicate player resource state to narrative elements. The game communicates tyranny through stage density and enemy composition, then world restoration through ending celebration cutscene. Life bar and checkpoint system provide granular danger feedback without explicit numerical HUD elements.
sources_used: tcrf (Dragon Spirit: The New Legend - The Cutting Room Floor)
wikipedia (Dragon Spirit - Wikipedia, covering arcade original and gameplay mechanics relevant to NES sequel)
